task_completion.description: Verify that the CT Cranium DICOM series is loaded into InVesalius 3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius 3 interface displays medical imaging views (axial, coronal, sagittal) of a human head or skull.

task_completion.description: Verify that the three required segmentation masks are created with the correct Hounsfield Unit (HU) thresholds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The masks panel in InVesalius shows multiple masks. Look for threshold settings corresponding to the task requirements: min 226 HU (bone), min 662 HU (compact bone), and max 225 HU (soft tissue).

task_completion.description: Verify that 3D surface meshes have been generated from the segmentation masks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer or the 'Surfaces' panel in InVesalius shows generated 3D models of the skull and tissues.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 3D surfaces are exported as STL files to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File save dialogs, a file manager window, or terminal output showing 'bone_tissue.stl', 'compact_bone.stl', and 'soft_tissue.stl' being saved to or present in the '/home/ga/Documents/tissue_exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the complete InVesalius project is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog, file manager window, or terminal output showing 'tissue_analysis.inv3' being saved to or present in the '/home/ga/Documents/tissue_exports/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to process the data and export files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the InVesalius interface (e.g., adjusting sliders, clicking export buttons) rather than relying solely on terminal commands or scripts to mock the outputs.

integrity.description: The generated masks and 3D surfaces correspond to actual anatomical structures.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer displays recognizable human cranial anatomy (skull, facial bones, soft tissue) rather than random noise, simple geometric shapes, or empty space.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the application by creating empty or dummy files.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent using terminal commands like 'touch' or a text editor to create the .stl or .inv3 files without actually generating them through InVesalius.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses a real CT Cranium DICOM dataset. The agent must use InVesalius 3 to create three masks with specific Hounsfield Unit (HU) thresholds: full bone (≥226 HU), compact cortical bone (≥662 HU), and soft tissue (≤225 HU). These masks must be converted to 3D surfaces and exported as STL files (bone_tissue.stl, compact_bone.stl, soft_tissue.stl) to /home/ga/Documents/tissue_exports/. The project must be saved as tissue_analysis.inv3 in the same directory. The 3D viewer should display recognizable human cranial anatomy.
